situation: Applied for a student visa at the German Embassy in Tokyo. The Fintiba blocked account held the correct balance, but the official certificate issued by Fintiba showed the previous year's required amount (€10,332) instead of the current 2026 requirement (€11,208).
rejection_reason: Blocked account certificate showed an amount below the current minimum required under the 2026 BAföG reference rate. Embassy denied the application citing §16 AufenthG insufficient financial proof.
what_worked: Contacted Fintiba support via live chat. They identified a document generation error and issued a corrected certificate within 24 hours. Resubmitted to the embassy and visa was approved in the next processing cycle.
what_failed: Arguing the old amount was sufficient based on when the account was opened. Submitting a bank statement showing additional personal savings to cover the gap. Trying to top up the account and generate a new certificate without first fixing the document error.
